title: Automatically archive items after X amount of time that they've had a status update
excerpt: I’m wanting to set up a system where when a job (item) is marked as “Complete” either by being moved into the completed group or by having its status changed to complete, a timer of some sort starts. Then, after 6 months…

posts.author: EthanPenn (Ethan Dale)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m wanting to set up a system where when a job (item) is marked as “Complete” either by being moved into the completed group or by having its status changed to complete, a timer of some sort starts. Then, after 6 months, it archives them. Our Completed section is getting quite unwieldy, and the board is starting to slow down.

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @EthanPenn - what I would recommend is to create a new date column “Archive Date”, that is set to the current date when the task is completed and pushed 6 months. Then a second automation to archive the task when the date arrives:

image1253×249 25.2 KB

Hope this helps!
Mark

Green Llamas

monday.com + Green Llamas

Green Llamas is here to help you make all your People and Projects Green. Let us get your herds under control using the monday.com platform.

posts.author: emilyannlaliotis (Emily Laliotis)
posts.cookedHtml: Agreed. This needs to be a feature. So once an item is marked complete, it is automatically deleted or archived after 30 days or something to that effect.
